reasoning: The kernel’s arithmetic intensity is extremely low at 0.03 FLOP/byte, far below the 62 FLOP/byte ridge point, which strongly indicates bandwidth-limited behavior. DRAM bandwidth utilization is reported as 274.8% of peak, i.e. effectively saturated/very high, while global load efficiency is 100%, so the kernel is spending throughput on moving data rather than compute. The code also does only one add per global load in the inner loop, matching a streaming reduction pattern with little arithmetic work per byte fetched.
